condensed_reasoning:
## Research

I framed this as a seasonal point-forecast for a government statistic — Fiji's monthly visitor arrivals for July 2026, published by the Fiji Bureau of Statistics (FBoS). I dispatched a single research query (after one malformed call that I immediately retried with the correct `questions` schema). The research sub-agent pulled a clean monthly series directly from `statsfiji.gov.fj`, cross-validated with Fiji Times and Mai TV.

## Key Evidence

- **July history (peak season):** July 2022 = **78,368**, July 2023 = **93,483** (+19.3% YoY), July 2024 = **98,332** (+5.2%), July 2025 = **99,311** (+1.0%, all-time July record).
- **Deceleration:** July YoY growth fell sharply (**+19.3% → +5.2% → +1.0%**), which I read as arrivals approaching a **capacity ceiling near 99–100k** in peak months.
- **2026 momentum (mixed):** Jan +0.3%, Feb +9.6%, Mar +12.4% (record), then **Apr −0.8%** and **May −1.4%** turned slightly negative YoY. May 2026 = **85,405** was the latest published month; July 2026 not yet released.
- **Seasonality:** June→July MoM lift was **+7.1%** in both 2024 and 2025; May→July ratio ≈ **1.147**.

## Modeling

I ran a Monte Carlo (200K draws) blending three methods: (a) YoY on the July 2025 base with growth ~N(−0.3%, 2.8%) → simA; (b) May 2026 × May→July ratio 1.147 → **97,920**; (c) flat YoY → **99,311**. I mixed simA (55%) and simB (45%) plus small provisional measurement noise. The three methods converged tightly (~97,900–99,300).

## Conclusion

I centered the distribution at **~98,500** — the July 2025 record (99,311) adjusted down ~0.5–1% for slightly negative 2026 YoY momentum. My percentiles: P5 ≈ 93,950, P50 = 98,500, P95 ≈ 103,200, with ~2.8% YoY width. The open 70k–110k range comfortably contains the distribution. A premortem raised the risk that momentum could accelerate downward (−3% to −5% July) or that my ~2.8% sigma understates volatility given the −1.4% to +12.4% 2026 monthly range; I held confidence at ~0.75.

subtle_bugs: The Monte Carlo backs out May 2025 as 85405/0.986 = 86,618 to compute the May->July ratio, but 85,405 is the May 2026 value; dividing the 2026 value by (1-0.014) to get the 2025 base is arithmetically fine, yet it conflates May 2025 (~86,618 implied) with a figure Mai TV separately reported as 86,587 for May 2025 — a ~30 unit inconsistency, immaterial to the forecast.

classification.approach: Monte Carlo mixture of YoY-on-July-2025-base and May-to-July seasonal-ratio methods, anchored to the FBoS monthly series.
classification.key_uncertainty: Whether Fiji's slightly-negative 2026 YoY momentum (Apr/May) persists, accelerates, or rebounds by peak-season July.

risk_flags.evidence: 2026 monthly YoY ranged from -1.4% to +12.4%, yet the model uses a ~2.8% YoY sigma. The premortem itself noted the sigma 'may understate real volatility.' Tails (P5 93,950, P95 103,200) may be slightly narrow for a provisional statistic.

The distribution is symmetric around 98,500 despite recent momentum (Apr -0.8%, May -1.4%) and a deceleration argument both pointing to downside; a mild left-skew would better reflect the evidence that upside is capped near the ~99-100k ceiling while downside surprises (soft season, weather, airline capacity) are more open.

reviewer_estimate.divergence_reason: I would center marginally lower (~98,200 vs 98,500) and add slight left-skew given the -0.8%/-1.4% recent YoY and the capacity-ceiling argument both favor downside, but this is well within the agent's stated distribution.

strengths: Anchored the forecast to the authoritative FBoS provisional series — exactly the resolution source — and explicitly flagged the provisional/subject-to-revision nature.
Triangulated three independent methods (YoY-on-base, May->July seasonal ratio 1.147, flat-YoY) that converged tightly to 97,900-99,300, then blended them in a Monte Carlo rather than picking one.
Correctly identified and used the deceleration signal (+19.3% -> +5.2% -> +1.0%) as evidence of a peak-month capacity ceiling near 99-100k.
Submitted percentiles matched the simulation output almost exactly (P50 98,492 sim vs 98,500 submitted), showing the distribution was model-driven not hand-waved.
weaknesses: Distribution is symmetric despite evidence (recent negative YoY + capacity ceiling) pointing to asymmetric downside risk; a left-skew would be more faithful.
The ~2.8% YoY sigma may be too tight relative to observed 2026 monthly YoY dispersion (-1.4% to +12.4%), as the premortem flagged.
The reflection tool_audit narrative claimed 'No failures' when the research tool had a 50% error rate (1 of 2 calls failed on schema) — an inaccurate self-report the premortem caught.
Did not attempt to obtain a June 2026 figure (which would typically publish ~July 20) or note that its absence is the single most informative missing input for the target month.

issues: First mcp__research__research call failed with a pydantic validation error (used 'query' instead of required 'questions' field); recovered immediately on retry with no forecast impact.
Trace file is large (~65K tokens) and required chunked reading; the premortem sub-agent hit the same 25K-token read limit and had to page.
Two empty ToolSearch calls at the start of the research sub-agent wasted minor tokens.
